text.title: CLAUDE.md Structure Rules
text.summary: Rules for what CLAUDE.md must contain, how long it should be, and what makes it useful versus harmful for AI-assisted development.

rule.statement: A CLAUDE.md must include: Commands (bash blocks for dev/test/lint/build), Structure (directory tree, 2 levels), Conventions (naming, imports, types), and Key Files (table of important entry points). Project Overview is optional but useful.
rule.rationale: Commands is the most frequently referenced section during AI-assisted development — agents need it to run tests, start servers, and apply linters. Without it, agents guess at command names and fail silently.

rule.statement: Keep CLAUDE.md under 150 lines total. A file over 300 lines consumes context budget that could be used for code.
rule.rationale: Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md at session start. Every line of CLAUDE.md is a line not available for the actual code the agent is working on. Concise routing beats comprehensive documentation.

antipattern.description: Too much information: documenting every edge case, every API endpoint, full architecture diagrams in prose.
antipattern.reason: Overwhelming context budget. Put detailed architecture docs in .agents/ and reference them from CLAUDE.md with a single line.

antipattern.description: Stale content: commands that no longer work, directory paths that no longer exist, env vars with wrong names.
antipattern.reason: Stale instructions mislead more than they help. An agent following a stale build command wastes tool calls diagnosing the failure rather than doing productive work.

antipattern.description: Duplicate docs: same information already in README.md reproduced in CLAUDE.md.
antipattern.reason: Creates a maintenance burden and inconsistency. CLAUDE.md should contain only what Claude needs that differs from what a human developer would look up in README.

antipattern.description: No structure: wall of text paragraphs without sections or headings.
antipattern.reason: Agents scan CLAUDE.md for specific sections. Unstructured prose requires parsing the entire file to find a specific command or convention.

antipattern.description: Wrong audience: written for human developers with background explanation and narrative prose.
antipattern.reason: CLAUDE.md is read by an agent, not a human onboarding. Omit explanations of why a technology was chosen; include only what the agent needs to operate correctly.

rule.statement: Review and update CLAUDE.md on every major PR that changes directory layout, build commands, or lint configuration.
rule.rationale: CLAUDE.md is a static snapshot. A 6-month-old file with wrong commands misleads every agent session until it is corrected. Tie updates to the same PR that changes the thing CLAUDE.md describes.

rule.statement: Use <required> notation for environment variables, never example values. Use SECRET_KEY=<required> not SECRET_KEY=abc123.
rule.rationale: Agents may use literal example values found in CLAUDE.md when generating code or configuration. Placeholder notation prevents accidental use of example credentials.

text.reference: Standard CLAUDE.md template for FastAPI/Django projects
Minimal CLAUDE.md for simple single-stack projects
Monorepo root CLAUDE.md delegating to per-module files
